task_completion.description: Open the stormdrain_design.xlsx workbook and site_land_use.csv data file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application is open showing the contents of stormdrain_design.xlsx and/or site_land_use.csv.

task_completion.description: Calculate the design flow using the Rational Method.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet shows a calculated design flow value of approximately 21.756 cfs.

task_completion.description: Calculate the full-flow capacity for the pipe catalog using Manning's Equation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet shows calculated pipe capacities, specifically around 16.04 cfs for a 24-inch pipe and 29.08 cfs for a 30-inch pipe.

task_completion.description: Record the smallest diameter that accommodates the design flow in the 'Selected_Diameter_in' field.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The value '30' is entered into the 'Selected_Diameter_in' field in the workbook.
Evidence of the file being saved, such as the agent clicking the 'Save' icon, using a save shortcut, or the absence of an 'unsaved changes' indicator in the application title bar.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the workbook using a spreadsheet GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc or Excel) is visible on the screen, rather than the agent using a Python script or terminal commands to modify the Excel file invisibly.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed the calculations rather than hardcoding the final answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Spreadsheet formulas are visible in the formula bar, or a calculator/scratchpad was used, rather than the agent instantly typing '21.756' or '30' without any visible computation steps.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct existing file rather than creating a new one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application title bar shows 'stormdrain_design.xlsx' and is saved to the Desktop, not a newly created file or a text document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: 1. Design flow (Rational Method) = 21.756 cfs. 2. Manning's capacity for 24-inch pipe = 16.04 cfs. 3. Manning's capacity for 30-inch pipe = 29.08 cfs. 4. Selected diameter = 30 inches (smallest pipe exceeding 21.756 cfs). 5. Manning's n = 0.013, Slope = 0.005.
